evidence: 5679 is a helper entered with HL already set by the caller; it sets DE=EF6D, C=0Eh, then BLOCK copies 15 bytes to EF6D..EF7B.
notes: The copied byte mapping is source+0 -> VV6D, source+1 -> VV6E, source+2 -> VV6F, and so on. This makes the normal carriage record index depend on the third byte of the restored state, masked by 5715h as VV6F & 07h.

evidence: 5676 sets HL=EF38 and falls into the 5679 helper, so it copies EF38..EF46 into EF6D..EF7B before testing VV6D.3 at 5695h.
notes: External traced callers at 25D6h, 25E7h, 288Ah, and 28AAh all set VV38.3 before calling 5676h, so their copied VV6D.3 is set and they take the 569Ah-56C5h path rather than the normal VV62=0 carriage scheduler at 56C8h.

evidence: 086Ah is the only traced caller of 563Ch. 563Ch clears VV6D bits 10h and 04h, sets VV6D bit 20h, ORs VV6F with 04h, derives EF79 from EF64, saves alternate-register pointers in EF75/EF77, sets EF70=001Bh, EF73=000Eh, VV72=00h, then enters 567Fh.
notes: Because this entry does not set VV6D.3, 5695h reaches 56C8h. Since 563Ch ORs VV6F.2 and no traced instruction changes VV6F before 5715h, the immediate 72B3h table index is constrained to 4..7.

evidence: 56C8 calls 5786, 56CB calls 5715, 56CE-56D3 copies five bytes from 72B3h+(VV6F&7)*5 to EF7C..EF80, and 56D4 clears VV81. 56D7 sets VV62=0, then chooses EF70 or EF73 as the EF64 countdown source and jumps with HL=7005h to 558Dh.
notes: If EF70 is nonzero, VV63 comes from VV72. If EF70 is zero, VV63 comes from the copied EF7C byte and EF64 comes from EF73. The copied EF7D/EF7E..EF80 bytes are then consumed by the FE1 TM1 reload path at 09AC-09BCh.

evidence: 28CE waits until VV82 equals 04h, computes FFB0h + 15*VV83, copies 15 bytes into EF38..EF46, then increments VV83 and wraps it at 5.
notes: The producer for the FFB0h runtime slots is not yet identified in the traced instruction set. A later 5676h call can schedule this restored template state.

evidence: 08AA decrements VV82, computes FFB0h + 15*VV84, calls 5679h to copy that slot directly into EF6D..EF7B, then increments VV84 and wraps it at 5.
notes: This is FE1-side restoration of queued scheduler state. 09E5-09F6 also probes the first byte of the VV84 slot at FFB0h+15*VV84, checking bit 08h against VV62==1 before returning status.
